entries.title: Trump Attends Dignified Transfer for U.S. Servicemembers Killed in Middle East
entries.description: It is the third such event that the president has attended since the start of the war with Iran.

entries.title: A strange transmissible cancer is spreading through the catfish in this lake
entries.description: For more than a decade, there's been something fishy going on in a lake that straddles Vermont and the province of Quebec. It involves cancer, a kind of catfish, and — possibly — clues about how tumors metastasize.

entries.title: June 1 trial date set for Venezuela's Nicolas Maduro in his U.S. drug trafficking case
entries.description: Former Venezuelan president Nicolas Maduro and his wife will go on trial on drug trafficking charges next June, a judge said Wednesday, as their lawyers continue to object to the U.S. military action that snatched them from power.

entries.title: National Guard deploys as flash floods in West Virginia inundate homes and businesses
entries.description: Torrential rains caused flash floods in West Virginia that inundated homes and businesses, washed out bridges and led to numerous water rescues, authorities said. The governor said there were no reports of deaths or missing people as of Wednesday.

entries.title: Portland ballot initiative to increase police staffing accused of misleading voters
entries.description: A Portland, Oregon, ballot initiative intended to divert surplus climate funds to boost police hiring has been dogged by accusations that canvassers are misleading voters to get their signatures -- an allegation proponents of the measure said is part of an organized campaign to taint their effort.

entries.title: Exclusive-Iran strikes on CIA facilities prompt questions about possible Russian role
entries.description: Exclusive-Iran strikes on CIA facilities prompt questions about possible Russian role

entries.title: 'The last picture': A passing boater's photograph may be the 5 adults who drowned in Ohio
entries.description: Marcus L. Martin felt instant trepidation Sunday when he saw five adults wading hip- and waist-deep in the Scioto River. A frequent boater on the Ohio waterway, the Baptist pastor knew the area where they were standing to be treacherous. He believes the people he captured in a photo to be the ill-fated group that drowned that day.

entries.title: AP Exclusive: A boater's photo may be 'the last picture' of group drowned in an Ohio river
entries.description: AP Exclusive: A boater's photo may be 'the last picture' of group drowned in an Ohio river

entries.title: Need backup power? Automakers want you to look no further than their vehicles in your driveway
entries.description: Winter Storm Uri, the multiday freeze that slammed Texas in February 2021 and pummeled the state's power grid, has been on Kenneth Kovar's mind ever since. Though the resident of New Braunfels, northeast of San Antonio, didn't lose power at the time, he wasn't able to run his septic tank - it is independent from local systems. He had to fill his toilets with water from his backyard pool.

entries.title: Ryan Gosling's 'La La Hand' is finally fixed in the new 'La La Land' poster
entries.description: Lionsgate has changed Ryan Gosling's flat hand gesture on the poster for 'La La Land' as the musical celebrates its 10th anniversary.

entries.title: 'Stuart Fails to Save the Universe' is an out-of-this-world comedy
entries.description: The latest spinoff from 'The Big Bang Theory' takes secondary characters and places them in a sci-fi adventure comedy filled with small details that bring the story alive.

entries.title: A year after Malcolm-Jamal Warner's death, widow Tenisha Warner sues actor's mother for $1.2 million
entries.description: Malcolm-Jamal Warner's widow and his mother are locked in a lawsuit over $1.2 million, a year after 'The Cosby Show' star accidentally drowned on vacation at age 54.

entries.description: Major League Baseball’s "Field of Dreams" game — set amid the cornfields of Iowa where Kevin Costner’s famed movie baseball field originated — will be available to watch live on Netflix in mid-August.

entries.title: Nolan Wells preliminary autopsy results released
entries.description: A forensic pathologist hired by the family of Nolan Wells says so far the cause and manner of death is undetermined. The autopsy was performed four days after his body was found. NBC News’ Aaron Gilchrist has the story.

entries.title: Angelina Jolie and Brad Pitt's daughter Vivienne asks court to drop her dad's last name
entries.description: Brad Pitt and Angelina Jolie's youngest daughter, Vivienne, is petitioning to drop her father's last name from her official name.

entries.title: Trump attends dignified transfer of U.S. troops
entries.description: The President was at Dover Air Force Base in Delaware today for the dignified transfer of four military personnel killed in recent days in the Middle East as attacks between the U.S. and Iran have escalated. One of the families of the fallen then flew home to Georgia aboard Air Force One with President Trump. NBC News’ Courtney Kube reports.

entries.title: After shocking quarter, IBM insists that AI isn’t killing the mainframe
entries.description: After IBM's stock crashed last week on warnings of poor mainframe sales, the CEO explained that AI wrecked corporate hardware budget, temporarily.

entries.title: Life Hack of the Day
entries.description: If you need to fill up a bucket that won't fit under your faucet, use a pool noodle. Set the bucket on the floor, slip the pool noodle over the faucet and run it down to the bucket.

entries.description: The GOP chair of the House Judiciary Committee encouraged the DOJ to look into criminally charging Jack Smith, alleging the former special counsel who brought charges against President Trump lied to Congress.

entries.title: Comforting news for coffee drinkers concerned about their heart health
entries.description: When it comes to heart health.. how many cups of caffeinated coffee are safe to drink each day? A new statement from the American Heart Association may be reassuring to coffee drinkers. Gwen Baumgardner has more.

entries.description: A California man is taking advantage of remote work with viral office settings that are attracting fans, critics, and thousands of views. Gwen Baumgarder joined him for a workday at the ballpark.

entries.title: Man shot and killed by police in Madison, Wisconsin, sparking protests
entries.description: Madison's chief of police says an officer in Wisconsin's capital city shot and killed a man who had injured him with a knife while resisting arrest.

entries.description: As the Lads head across the Atlantic, we asked our writers for their thoughts on what they’d like to see!

entries.title: Felipe Mora scores in stoppage time to help Timbers escape with 2-2 draw against Dallas
entries.description: Felipe Mora scored two minutes into stoppage time to erase a late go-ahead goal by Dallas' Petar Musa and help the Portland Timbers earn a 2-2 draw on Wednesday night.  Mora took a pass from Kristoffer Velde before scoring his second goal of the season for the equalizer for Portland (5-8-3).  Velde subbed into the match in the 55th minute before posting his career-high third assist.

entries.title: Al Hilal agree deal with West Ham for Crysencio Summerville
entries.description: Al Hilal have agreed a deal with West Ham United to sign winger Crysencio Summerville, according to The Athletic.Both clubs have agreed a deal worth an initial £55 million, with a further £5m in add...

entries.description: Here's everything you need to know about the 2026 WNBA All-Star Weekend, including TV channel and streaming options for each event.

entries.title: Is 2026 the Commonwealth Games' last dance or a future-proofing revamp?
entries.description: It's been going for 96 years - but many doubted the 2026 Commonwealth Games, which open in Glasgow on Thursday, would happen. So what could the future hold for the event?

entries.description: Interview: Having hung up her wheels, Britain’s most successful Paralympian is now turning to punditry, and believes that despite a difficult build-up Glasgow will put the Commonwealth Games back on the map

entries.title: Are the Commonwealth Games dying?
entries.description: The Games return this week but spiralling costs, a slimmed-down programme and the weight of its uncomfortable history all put the future of the event, approaching its centenary year, in doubt

entries.title: Two waste paper bins, one Olympic dream
entries.description: Derived from basketball and developed by teenagers in late-Victorian England, netball is sport's great misunderstood "girly game". It could be heading to the Olympics in 2032.

entries.title: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket is expected to crash into the moon this August
entries.description: The upper stage of a Falcon 9 rocket has been drifting in space for over a year after it launched. It's expected to crash into the moon next month.

entries.title: Dubai’s DP World to Build Two New Terminals on U.A.E. East Coast, Bypassing Hormuz
entries.description: The project, agreed in principle with the Fujairah Ports Authority under a 50-year concession, includes a container and multipurpose terminal and a general-cargo terminal.

entries.title: Bears Are Running Amok in Lake Tahoe and Causing Chaos Among Locals
entries.description: Homeowners spend thousands to ward off the pantry-raiding, toothpaste-sniffing beasts, exposing a rift among volunteer groups.

entries.title: Google Has the Muscle to Overpower Spending Worries
entries.description: AI investment and delays are concerns, but the company’s core businesses look strong.

entries.title: Video shows an orca torpedoing toward a sunfish, blowing it into pieces
entries.description: Video shows one orca holding a sunfish by the fin and another torpedoing toward it, blowing it into pieces in the Gulf of California.

entries.title: US on track to have historically low number of homicides in 2026, report says
entries.description: A new report from the Council on Criminal Justice shows an 18% decrease in the homicide rate across 30 American cities from 2025 compared to the same period in 2026

entries.title: Google adds selfie video as a log-in option
entries.description: You'll now be able to get into your Google account with a selfie video.

entries.description: Sony has just unveiled its latest cinema camera that may foreshadow some long-awaited features in its consumer mirrorless lineup.

entries.description: Plus, the U.S. gives the president more muscular military options as he considers expanding the Iran war, and we go inside Taco Bell’s rush to contain cyclospora.

entries.description: Plus, is oil headed back above $100?

entries.title: Number of cyclosporiasis cases in the U.S. has surpassed previous records, CDC says
entries.description: It can take weeks to confirm a single case of intestinal illness caused by the cyclospora parasite, but the CDC's tally for this year now stands at over 4,100.

entries.title: Blackstone’s Profit Surges on AI Investments
entries.description: Stronger inflows into the investment firm’s private-equity business offset a slowdown in private credit, where individual investors have pulled back.

entries.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Pushes West After Battering Louisiana
entries.description: Tropical Storm Bertha is pushing toward Texas after crashing ashore in Louisiana. The Gulf Coast was hammered with heavy rain, high winds and floods. Meanwhile, some areas are set to face triple digit temperatures through the weekend. NBC’s George Solis reports and TODAY’s Al Roker tracks the latest forecast.

entries.title: Why health officials do not have a good handle on the current Ebola outbreak
entries.description: To get slightly ahead of the virus, health officials need to quickly identify people at risk of infection, a practice known as contact tracing. That's a challenge in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

entries.title: New Concerns Over Oil Prices After Houthis Attack Saudi Tankers
entries.description: There are new concerns about the future of oil and gas prices after Yemen-based Houthi rebels, backed by Iran, attacked two Saudi Arabian oil tankers in the Red Sea. NBC’s Richard Engel reports on the new front in the Middle East conflict and Christine Romans joins TODAY with analysis on the economic impact to gas prices in the United States.

entries.title: This AI tool promises a 'second sight of eyes' to clinicians. Did patients benefit?
entries.description: Medical workers in a Nairobi clinic used an AI tool to check their work. A new study evaluates the result.

entries.title: This AI tool promises a 'second pair of eyes' to clinicians. Did patients benefit?
entries.description: Medical workers in a Nairobi clinic used an AI tool to check their work. A new study evaluates the result.

entries.title: OpenAI says rogue AI models broke free from human control. Some see it as a 'warning shot'
entries.description: It is the kind of development once seen only in science fiction: An artificial intelligence system, trained to probe for digital vulnerabilities, breaks free of human control and acts on its own to hack another company

entries.title: Man who pleaded guilty to killing top Minnesota Democratic lawmaker and her husband to be sentenced
entries.description: The Minnesota man who pleaded guilty to killing a top Democratic state lawmaker and her husband is due to be sentenced in federal court

entries.title: Protests Erupt After Fatal Police Shooting of Man in Wisconsin
entries.description: An investigation is underway after a police officer in Madison, Wisconsin, fatally shot a man who investigators say pulled a knife during a scuffle with deputies. Police say it took place after the man fled from officers who were responding to a report of an individual allegedly stealing bicycles and checking car doors. NBC’s Shaquille Brewster reports for TODAY. Warning: Video of the incident is disturbing.

entries.title: Trump says Saudi nuclear pact requires kingdom to normalize Israel relations through Abraham Accords
entries.description: President Donald Trump says the Saudi nuclear agreement requires the kingdom to normalize relations with Israel through the Abraham Accords

entries.title: Trump says Saudi Arabia must normalize Israel ties before nuclear deal is approved
entries.description: President Donald Trump said Thursday that a nuclear deal between the United States and Saudi Arabia requires the kingdom to normalize relations with Israel — a significant condition not previously mentioned as the deal was announced.

entries.title: Get into med school in the graphic memoir 'See One, Do One, Teach One'
entries.description: Grace Farris' book brings readers behind the med school scenes — documenting anxiety over loans, bouts of impostor syndrome, friendships forged in stress, and the ever-impossible work-life balance.

entries.title: FDA Advisory Committee to Review Peptide Restrictions Underway
entries.description: Peptides are often being promoted by wellness influencers and podcasters as treatments for everything from injuries and aging and now an FDA panel is meeting to discuss whether to loosen restrictions and make them easier to get. But critics say there's not enough data on safety and efficacy. NBC’s medical contributor Natalie Azar joins TODAY to break down the seven peptides being discussed and how effective they are.

entries.title: Your Guide to (Still) Eating Fresh Fruits and Vegetables This Summer
entries.description: An outbreak of cyclosporiasis that has sickened thousands shouldn’t prevent you from eating any fresh produce, experts say.

entries.title: Wisconsin governor calls for transparency after Madison officer fatally shoots man with knife
entries.description: The police shooting death of a man with a knife has roiled a Wisconsin city and prompted the governor to call for transparency and accountability in any investigation

entries.title: White House says Saudi Arabia nuclear deal contingent on joining Abraham Accords
entries.description: During a press briefing, White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt echoed a social media post from President Trump that said any nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia would be contingent on them joining the Abraham Accords. NBC News' Monica Alba reports from the White House on the potential deal and the White House's approach.

entries.title: Member of Vance’s Secret Service detail removed amid probe into alleged compromise of security info
entries.description: A member of JD Vance’s protective detail has been removed pending an investigation into allegations of compromising the vice president’s security, a Secret Service spokesman said Thursday.

entries.title: Fan-fave 'Love Island USA' sweethearts Olandria, Nic Vansteenberghe call it quits
entries.description: The summer sun has set on 'Love Island USA' fan favorites Olandria and Nic Vansteenberghe's romance, a year after their time on the hit series.

entries.title: How did 'Drive find that higher gear? The director says it was thanks to 'Sixteen Candles'
entries.description: For its 15th anniversary, 'Drive' director Nicolas Winding Refn looks back on working with Ryan Gosling, shooting in L.A. and some surprising influences.

entries.title: Despite tax credits, filming in L.A. is still sluggish
entries.description: The number of shoot days from incentivized productions is slowly growing, but Los Angeles' on-location filming is still down compared to last year.

entries.title: Hot faces fill the erotic thriller 'Her Private Hell,' but the plot is never in focus
entries.description: 'Drive' director Nicolas Winding Refn's first feature in a decade finds him still fixated on sizzling neon and chilly characters. He's got his kinks.

entries.title: Consecutive Life Sentences in Minnesota Lawmaker Killings
entries.description: Vance Luther Boelter pleaded guilty to shooting two state Democratic lawmakers, killing Rep. Melissa Hortman and her husband.

entries.title: Justice Department Agrees to Withdraw Subpoenas for New York Times Journalists
entries.description: A federal judge questioned whether prosecutors followed proper procedures in a criminal leak investigation.

entries.title: The Uplift: The American Dream
entries.description: We celebrate the United States ahead of its 250th anniversary with stories about American heroes and pastimes. The founder of e.l.f. Cosmetics shares how he made a massive life change to become a Catholic priest.
